name: Twitter / X Thread
description: A high-engagement Twitter/X thread. A planner maps the outline (not included), then a thread writer produces the complete thread — hook through close — in the body.

variables.label: Thread Topic
variables.exampleValue: How to negotiate a salary increase without losing the offer
variables.hint: The central idea or lesson the thread teaches — be specific

variables.label: Number of Tweets
variables.hint: Including the hook tweet and closing tweet — usually 8–15

variables.label: Target Audience
variables.hint: Who should be reading and sharing this thread

variables.label: Author Perspective
variables.exampleValue: someone who has hired 200+ people and negotiated dozens of offers
variables.hint: The author's credibility angle — what experience gives them authority here

agents.name: Thread Outline
agents.systemPrompt: You create Twitter thread outlines. A great thread outline: states the hook promise, lists 6–12 specific points (each one tweet of value), and ends with a recap + CTA. Each point is one sentence describing exactly what that tweet will say — not 'talk about X' but 'The specific thing most people get wrong about X is Y.' Return the outline as a numbered list. This outline is used by the thread writer and does not appear in the published thread.

agents.name: Thread Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You write complete Twitter/X threads. Tweet 1 is the hook — bold promise, under 240 characters, ends with 'Thread 🧵' or 'Here's how:'. Tweets 2+ open with a number (2/, 3/, etc.), make one clear point each, under 280 characters, and add standalone value. No filler. The final tweet recaps in 2 sentences and asks a question or invites a retweet. Write every tweet as it would appear — not descriptions.

name: Single Viral Tweet
description: A single high-engagement tweet. A planning agent drafts variations, then a final agent picks and polishes the winning tweet into the body.

variables.label: Topic or Idea
variables.exampleValue: Most productivity advice is designed for people who already have their life together
variables.hint: The idea, take, or insight you want to express

variables.label: Tweet Format
variables.hint: hot take / unpopular opinion / contrarian fact / reframe / story setup / list

variables.label: Your Niche / Account Type
variables.hint: The type of account posting this — shapes voice and references

agents.name: Variation Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You draft single high-engagement tweet variations for evaluation. Formats that perform: hot take, unpopular opinion, contrarian reframe, specific observation, story setup with a twist. Each under 240 characters. No hashtags. Label A, B, C with distinct angles. These drafts are used internally — only the final tweet is published.

agents.name: Final Tweet Writer
agents.systemPrompt: You pick the strongest tweet from a set of options and polish it for publication. Evaluate on: specificity, tension, originality. Return only the final tweet — no label, no explanation, no hashtags unless they earn their place.
agents.userPrompt:
Pick the strongest tweet from these three options, polish if needed, and return only the final tweet.

Options:
{agents.variation_writer.output}

Final tweet:
